entry.title: How to publish data from Comp info to Job info?
entry.content:
Scenario: There is a field Sub Role Band on the job info. An employee Sub Role Band changes during the compensation cycle. How can we publish this new value on the job info?

Resolution: It can be done by making a table report of final value after the compensation cycle and this report needs to be hosted on SFTP server and from there the value will be pulled with the help of integration on the job info field.

Following is the detailed reproduction of issue as well as the solution.

Employee’s Current Sub Role Band:

Change of Sub Role Band during ongoing compensation cycle. Screenshot of the final worksheet:

Now, we have to make a table report of the value (Final Pseudo Role Band) that need to be pushed from compensation to job info.

Steps to be followed for making a report:

Go to Report Centre. Click on New > Table Report > Single Domain > Compensation Planning.

Following is the screenshot of the report. Here for a user both the current and final values have been taken.

Steps For Scheduling a Report:

  • Click on three dots in the right side of the corresponding report.

  • Click on New Schedule. We get four sections to fill:
  • Schedule Definition
  • Filter
  • Destination
  • Schedule Occurrence

  • Give the name of the schedule. Select the file format. Here it is selected as csv. Choose the notification settings if required.
  • Click on step 2. There is nothing to choose at this step. Click on Step 3.
  • Here we give details about the SFTP server where this report file is going to be hosted.

 

  • Select FTP as the method. Then click on the checkbox saying SF hosted SFTP server and provide the credentials. Now provide the file path that is in which folder of SFTP we want to push the report.
  • Here the folder is /incoming. Also provide the file name containing the format as selected during Schedule definition. If we want to include the date format in our file name then select from the format as provided in the dropdown. Though it is not mandatory. After that click on Step 4.

  • Select the frequency of our schedule. Here it is selected as once as the final value is captured after the completion of compensation cycle. Then we need to select the date and time of our first occurrence.

  • After the successful completion of scheduling, the report will appear in the destination folder of SFTP server:

  • Next step is to create an integration to pull this report. Following are the steps:

Search Integration Center > Click on Create > Click on More Integration Types > Choose Integration Type

 

 

Select the options as below:

  • Trigger Type: Scheduled
  • Source Type: SFTP as our file is on SFTP
  • Destination Type: SuccessFactors (Value needs to be reflected on jobInfo portlet)
  • Format: CSV

Click on Create. Then search for job information in the search bar as we need to map the fields on the job information with our report fields. Next click on select.

 

  • In the options setting, select the integration name and write the description if needed. Then click on Next and move to the configure fields section.
  • Here we need to first upload a sample report file in the Sample Data View.

  • Click on Field Mapping View. Apart from mapping our destination field, we need to map certain mandatory fields (User Id, Start Date and Sequence Number).

  • We have to drag and drop User ID from source settings on to the User ID in destination fields to map them.
  • Similarly Final Pseudo Role Band is mapped.
  • Next, click on the Field Detail View and select the default value of sequence number.

  • Now select the start date of the integration. Here it is selected as Today. Else we can set calculation when we want the integration to start.

  • Select “Today” from the dropdown in front of the field value. Click ok.

  • After all the fields are mapped click on Next and configure the Source Settings.

  • File Server Settings to be configured as follows:
  • Enter the SFTP host address.
  • 22 is the default SFTP port.
  • In the authentication type select Basic Authentication.
  • Enter the SFTP credentials.
  • File Name and Folder Settings to be configured as follows:
  • Enter the name of the file (Hosted on SFTP server through Reporting Center) without file format.
  • Date suffix format to be selected if the file name contains the date.
  • Select the file extension as selected while scheduling the report.
  • Select the source folder of the SFTP.
  • Click on Next and set the schedule of the integration.

 

  • Email id can be mentioned if we want a notification for failed integrations. Then click on Next.

  • We need to click on Save once all the settings are configured. Then we can click on Run Now if we want to run the integrations or we can click on Set Schedule if we want the integration to run on our predefined scheduled date and time.
  • After that we can check the status of our integration from Last run time or by following the below steps:
  • Search Integration Center > Monitor Integration > Select the integration to see the details or errors if any.
  • Once the integration runs successfully, we need to check the value of our required field (Sub Role Band) in jobinfo.
  • Attached is the screenshot of jobinfo history notifying the change in value of Sub Role Band.

 

Regards,

Farhan Danish

entry.title: SAP EWM - Multiple Auto Pick HU Creation for Single Warehouse Order
entry.content:
This blog shows demonstration of how individual Pick HU can be created for each Warehouse task in a single Warehouse Order. 

Business Scenario: For a Single Warehouse order with a multiple warehouse task, each warehouse task should have an individual Pick HU created automatically. 

Configuration:

  • Create WOCR (Warehouse Order Creation Rule)
  1. Creation of Limits: SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Warehouse Order > Define Limit Values for the Size of a Warehouse Order

2. Creation of Packing Profile: SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Warehouse Order > Define Packing Profile for Warehouse Order Creation

3. Creation of WO:  SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Warehouse Order > Define Creation Rules for Warehouse Orders

4. Determination of WOCR: SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Warehouse Order >Define Search Sequence of Creation Rules for Activity Areas

  • Assignment of Pick HU Procedure

SCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Define Warehouse Number Control

Select warehouse number and assign procedure.

Master Data:

  • Creation of Packaging Specification (/N/SCWM/PACKSPEC)
  1. Click on Create button and Select correct Packaging Specification group. ￼
  2. Enter the correct Packaging material
  3. Maintain condition records with a correct condition type.
  • Assign Start/End Storage Bin for Activity Area (/N/SCWM/SEBA)

 

Testing:

  • Create Sale Order (VA01)
  • Create Outbound Delivery (VL01N)

  • Open Outbound Delivery in EWM (/N/SCWM/PRDO)

  • Create Warehouse Task/ Warehouse Order

  • Open and check warehouse order for Created Pick Hus

               3 Warehouse tasks are created under one Warehouse order.

              We can observe Automatic Pick HUs are created for each individual Warehouse task.

We can observe that the destination HUs automatically assigned to each Warehouse task. We can confirm the warehouse task and take print of each HU label and stick HU labels.

  • Confirm Warehouse Order

                   Once all the warehouse tasks confirmed, WO will be confirmed.

entry.title: SAP EWM - Customer Returns with Quality Inspection
entry.content:
Introduction :

We use this business process to handle customer returns. When you receive the rejected goods, We perform a complete inspection. Depending on the inspection result, We decide if the goods are put away or scrapped.

First of all, the customer creates a returns order with reference to the invoice. Then the returns delivery is created. The customer sends back the rejected goods. A truck with the returned goods arrives at a door where it is unloaded. The returns delivery is packed and the goods receipt is posted to create a Quality Inspection Document. The Quality Work Center is used to do the quality inspection including the quality inspection and the decision with follow-up action. On the one hand, the putaway can be done, on the other hand, products can be moved to the scrap zone.

This process does not support the following functions:

  • Unauthorized returns
  • Follow-up actions such as Sending goods back to the customer and Refurbishing

 

  1.    Creating Outbound Delivery Orders without Order References in SAP ERP

Use

In this step, you create an outbound delivery order.

Procedure

Access the transaction using one of the following navigation options:

SAP Menu

Logistics → Logistics Execution → Outbound Process → Goods Issue for Outbound Delivery → Outbound Delivery → Create → Single Document → Without Order Reference

Transaction Code

VL01NO

 

On the Create Outbound Delivery Order Without Order Reference screen, create the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Shipping Point

1111

 

 

Delivery Type

LO

 

 

Sales Organization

1000

 

 

Distribution Channel

10

 

 

Division

10

 

 

 

Choose Enter.

On the Create Delivery w/o Ref. Create: Overview screen, create the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Ship-to party

EWMCUST001

 

 

Planned GI

Current date

 

 

Material

<any material for example EWMPROD-S05>

 

 

Deliv. Qty

for example 6

 

 

Plant

E100

 

 

Storage Location

YAFS

 

 

 

Choose Enter.

Choose Save.

Result

You have created an outbound delivery order without reference. Delivery Number created is 80000782.

 

  1.    Selecting Outbound Delivery Orders

Use

In this step, you select the outbound delivery order in SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM).

Procedure

  1. Access the transaction using one of the following navigation options:

SAP Menu

Extended Warehouse Management → Delivery Processing → Outbound Delivery → Maintain Outbound Delivery Order

Transaction Code

/N/SCWM/PRDO

 

 

  1. On the Maintain Outb. Del. Order – Warehouse No. W100 screen, create the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Criteria for Simple Search

REFDOCNO_ERP_I

ERP Document

 

ERP Outbound Delivery

80000782

 

 

 

 

  1. Choose Execute.       

 

 

  1. Note down the SAP EWM Outbound Delivery Order which is 202 in this case.

Result

You have noted down the outbound delivery order number 202 in SAP EWM.

 

  1.    Confirming Warehouse Tasks

Use

In this step, you confirm the warehouse task, which is automatically created in the SAP EWM Warehouse Management Monitor.

Procedure

  1. Access the transaction using one of the following navigation options:

SAP Menu

Extended Warehouse Management → Monitoring → Warehouse Management

Transaction Code

/N/SCWM/MON

 

 

 

 

  1. In the Warehouse Management Monitor dialog box, choose Outbound – Documents – Outbound Delivery Order and choose Outbound Delivery Order.

     

  2.  

  1. Enter the SAP EWM Outbound Delivery Order:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Outb. Delivery Order

202

 

 

 

 

  1. Choose Execute.
  2. Choose Warehouse Order. Warehouse Order 374 is displayed.
 

  1. Select the Warehouse Order 374, choose More Methods – Confirm Backgr.
  2. Confirm the information screen.

     

 

 

 

Result

You have confirmed the picking warehouse task 373 in SAP EWM.

 

 

  1.    Maintaining Outbound Delivery Orders

Use

In this step, you maintain the outbound delivery order in SAP EWM.

Procedure

Access the transaction using one of the following navigation options:

SAP Menu

Extended Warehouse Management → Delivery Processing → Outbound Delivery → Maintain Outbound Delivery Order

Transaction Code

/N/SCWM/PRDO

 

On the Maintain Outb. Del. Order – Warehouse No. W100  screen, create the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Criteria for Simple Search

REFDOCNO_ERP_I

ERP Document

 

ERP Outbound Delivery

80000782

 

 

 

 

Choose Execute.

Select the SAP EWM Outbound Delivery Order 202.

Choose Goods Issue.

Result

You have posted the Goods Issue for the outbound delivery order in SAP EWM.

 

  1.    Processing Billing Due Lists in SAP ERP

Use

In this step, you process the billing due list in SAP ERP.

Procedure

  1. Access the transaction using one of the following navigation options:

SAP Menu

Logistics → Sales and Distribution → Shipping and Transportation → Billing → Billing Document → Process Billing Due List

Transaction Code

VF04

 

  1. On the Maintain Billing Due List screen, create the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Billing Date from

<Current date>

13.01.2013

 

  1. Choose DisplayBillList

 

  1. Select the relevant document and choose Individual billing document.

 

  1. Choose Save.
 

Result

You have processed the billing document in SAP ERP. Billing Document number generated is 90000817.

 

  1.    Creating Returns Deliveries

Use

In this step, you create a returns delivery in SAP ERP.

Procedure

  1. Access the transaction using one of the following navigation options:

SAP Menu

Logistics → Sales and Distribution → Sales → Order → Create

Transaction Code

VA01

 

  1. On the Create Sales Order: Initial Screen screen, create the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Order Type

RE

 

 

Sales Organization

1000

 

 

Distribution Channel

10

 

 

Division

10

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Choose Create with Reference.
 

 

 

  1. On the BillDoc tab page, create the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Billing Document

90000817

 

 

 

  1. Choose Copy.

 

  1. On the Create Returns: Overview screen, create the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Payment terms

0001

 

 

Order reason

Poor Quality

 

 

Incoterms

FH

 

 

Order Quantity

5

 

 

 

  1. Choose Enter.
  2. Confirm the information message.
  3. Choose Save.

Result

You have created a returns delivery in SAP ERP. Returns Order created is 60000142 and Returns Delivery created is 84000090

 

  1.   Packing Inbound Deliveries and Posting Goods Receipts

Use

In this step, you check that the truck has arrived, and you pack the inbound delivery.

Procedure

  1. Access the transaction using one of the following navigation options:

SAP Menu

Extended Warehouse Management → Delivery Processing → Inbound Delivery → Maintain Inbound Delivery

Transaction Code

/N/SCWM/PRDI

 

 

 

  1. On the Maintain Inbound Delivery – Warehouse Number W100 screen, create the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Criteria for Simple Search

ERP Document

 

 

ERP Document

84000090

Enter the previously created returns delivery number

 

 

 

  1. Choose Execute.
  2. Select Inbound Delivery Item and choose Switch to Form View. Check that the Warehouse Door DI01 and the Warehouse Process Type P430 are found.
 

 

 

 

 
  1. Select Inbound Delivery and choose Inbound Delivery – Follow-On Functions – Pack.
 

 

 

 

  1. On the Work Center Packing for Inbound Delivery screen, create the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Pack. Material

CARTON_S

The product fits into the packaging material

 

 

 

 

  1. Choose Execute.

 

 

 

  1. Drag and drop your product into the new HU 8000000020.
 

 

 

 

  1. Choose Save.
  2. Choose Back.
  3. Select Inbound Delivery and choose Goods Receipt.
 

 

 

 

Result

The delivery is packed and the goods receipt is directly posted to the quality inspection work center.

 

  1.    SAP EWM Quality Inspection

Use

In this step, you do the quality inspection and indirect counting for the returned delivery including decisions and follow-up actions.

Procedure

  1. Access the transaction using one of the following navigation options:

SAP Menu

Extended Warehouse Management → Execution → Quality Inspection and Count

Transaction Code

/N/SCWM/QINSP

 

 

 

 

 

  1. On the Work Center: Quality Inspection and Counting screen, create the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Warehouse Number

W100

 

 

Work Center

WQ01

 

 

Handling Unit

8000000020

 

 

 

 

  1. Choose Execute.
 

 

 

  1. Double Click on the Product EWMPROD-S01 and choose the Qual. Insp. tab page.
 

 

  1. On the Qual. Inspection tab page, choose Add Inspection Document Item.
 

  1. On the one hand, enter the quantity 3, and the decision code DCRA for the item, which has to be put away. On the other hand, enter the quantity 2, and the decision code DCRR for the item, which has to be scrapped.
 

  1. Choose Finding Creation: Findings were not yet created for the item to be scrapped only.
 

  1. On the Entry of Finding screen, choose Add Finding and enter the code A.
 

  1. Choose Transfer.
 

 

  1. Choose Save.
 

 

  1. Choose Back.

Result

You have done the quality inspection and indirect counting for the returned delivery. Depending on the decision code, the items are scrapped or put away.

 

  1.    Putaway

Use

In this step, you do the putaway of the relevant items.

Procedure

  1. Access the transaction using one of the following navigation options:

SAP Menu

Extended Warehouse Management → Execution → Confirm Warehouse Task

Transaction Code

/N/SCWM/TO_CONF

 

  1. On the Confirm Warehouse Task in warehouse Number W100 screen, make the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Criteria for Simple Search

TANUM

 

 

Warehouse Task

374

Enter the warehouse task, which was created in the Quality Work Center

 

 

  1. Choose Confirm + Save to move the stock to Storage Bin T051.
 

 

Result

You confirmed the Putaway warehouse task 374, which was created in the quality work center.

 

  1. Create Warehouse Task to move the stock to scrap zone

Use

In this step, you create Warehouse Task to move the stock to scrap zone.

Procedure

  1. Access the transaction using one of the following navigation options:

SAP Menu

Extended Warehouse Management → Work Scheduling → Create Warehouse Task without Reference

Transaction Code

/N/SCWM/ADPROD

 

 

  1. On the Create Product Warehouse Task in Warehouse Number W100 screen, create the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Find

Product

 

 

Product

EWMPROD-S01

 

 

 

 

  1. Choose Execute.
  2. Select the relevant item, by Source HU No. 8000000020, which should be moved to Scrap Zone.
 

  1. Click on “Switch to Form View”
 

 

  1. Enter the Src Target Qty as 2 and Warehouse Process Type as P425 and Press “Enter”.
 

 

  1. Choose “Create + Save”.
 

Result

You have moved the stock to the scrap zone. Warehouse Task 380 gets created and confirmed in the backend.

  1.                Scrapping

Use

In this step, you do the scrapping of the relevant items.

Procedure

  1. Access the transaction using one of the following navigation options:

SAP Menu

Extended Warehouse Management → Execution → Post Unplanned Goods Issue

Transaction Code

/N/SCWM/ADGI

 

 

 

 

  1. On the Post Unplanned Goods Issue screen, create the following entries:

Field Name

Value

User Action and Values

Comment

Warehouse Number

W100

 

 

GI Process

SCRP

Scrapping

 

  1. Choose Execute.
 

  1. Select the relevant item, for example by GR Date, which should be scrapped.
 

  1. Choose Post GI.
 

  1. Material Document 100000000208 is created.

Result

You have scrapped the stock, which is moved to the scrap zone after the quality inspection decision.

 

Thanks

MUNAFF. J

entry.title: Maximizing Efficiency with SAP YL and SAP PM Integration
entry.content:
Introduction:

Efficient yard operations and well-maintained equipment are critical for seamless logistics and supply chain performance. In this blog, we explore how integrating SAP Yard Logistics (YL) with SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) can unlock significant business benefits by streamlining yard operations and ensuring asset reliability.

SAP YL empowers businesses to optimize and manage yard logistics, enhancing operational visibility and efficiency. Meanwhile, SAP PM focuses on maintaining plant equipment and machinery, ensuring they are always in peak condition. By bringing these two powerful solutions together, organizations can:

  • Minimize Operational Delays: Ensure yard assets, such as vehicles, cranes, and machinery, are available and in top condition to avoid disruptions in logistics.
  • Enhance Asset Lifecycle Management: Proactively monitor and maintain equipment to reduce unexpected breakdowns and increase operational reliability.
  • Optimize Resource Utilization: Align maintenance schedules with yard logistics demands for seamless equipment availability.

This blog demonstrates the seamless integration between SAP YL and SAP PM, offering insights into the underlying processes and showcasing how businesses can achieve a smoother, more resilient supply chain with better resource management and minimized risks.

Prerequisites:

The fundamental yard setup, yard structure and Plant Maintenance setup should already be configured. Here we are mainly focusing on the integration part.

SAP Yard Logistics Configuration:

Assign PM Workorder Content Structure to a Yard Number and Storage Type

This setup helps you link maintenance tasks to the right locations in the yard, so everything runs smoothly and efficiently.

Assign PM Equipment Category to a Yard Number

The purpose of assigning a PM Equipment Category to a Yard Number is to make sure that maintenance tasks are linked to the right equipment in a specific yard location making maintenance and tracking easier.

Set Activity Type relevant for PM Integration

Activity relevancy for PM is needed so that Yard Logistics (YL) knows that when this task is executed, it requires integration with the Plant Maintenance (PM) system.

As a technical requirement for proper integration between SAP YL and SAP PM, the transportation unit name must not include any underscores, as the equipment name on the PM side does not support these characters.

SAP Plant Maintenance Configuration:

Create Functional Location Structure Indicator for SAP Yard Logistics for SAP S/4HANA

This setup helps to identify each asset’s location and ensure it gets the right maintenance when needed.

Scenario: 

In this blog, I have focused on an Outbound Rail scenario. After a train with several individual railcars is checked in and inspected, it is found that one of the railcar units is damaged and needs repairs before it can be loaded. In SAP YL, a task related to Plant Maintenance (PM) is created. Once the PM-related yard task is activated, a corresponding PM work order is automatically generated. After confirming the active PM-related yard task, the linked PM work order is released, the transportation unit is moved to a designated repair area, and the transportation unit is locked within SAP YL.

Once the integrated PM workorder is technically completed, the relevant transportation unit is unlocked and the SAP YL process can proceed.

Note: If the PM relevant yard task is cancelled, linked PM workorder will be marked as cancelled and the deletion flag will be set.

Execution:

Assume that the railcar has already been checked in and is awaiting inspection.

During the inspection, it is discovered that a railcar unit is damaged and unsuitable for loading the product. As a result, the yard operator selects the appropriate options from the questionnaire and confirms the task.

An ad-hoc task relevant to SAP PM is created and activated based on the results of the questionnaire.

After the task is activated, a workorder is generated in SAP PM, and the corresponding notification can be found in the SLG1 logs.

The generated workorder can be viewed using the Fiori tile ‘Find Maintenance Orders’.

Once yard task is confirmed, railcar unit is moved to damaged siding storage bin for repair and a notification to release workorder is sent to SAP PM.

Once the workorder is released, the user must enter the required details in the workorder to assign tasks to workers. This can be done using the Fiori tile ‘Change Maintenance Orders’.

After the workers have repaired the railcar unit, confirmation of the completed work can be provided using the Fiori tile ‘Perform Maintenance Jobs.

Now using the Fiori tile ‘Change Maintenance Orders’, the workorder can be technically completed.

 

 

Once the integrated PM workorder is technically completed, the relevant transportation unit is unlocked, and the SAP YL process can proceed.

The railcar unit can now be moved from the damaged siding storage bin and continue with the loading process.

Conclusion:

To sum up, the integration of SAP Yard Logistics (SAP YL) and SAP Plant Maintenance (SAP PM) provides an efficient solution for managing both yard operations and maintenance activities. By automating key processes such as task creation, workorder handling, and equipment upkeep, businesses can minimize downtime, boost operational efficiency, and optimize asset usage.

This integration enhances visibility and coordination between logistics and maintenance teams, resulting in improved resource management, timely maintenance, and more seamless operations overall. Whether you're managing outbound rail shipments, overseeing maintenance tasks, or looking to optimize your logistics processes, the integration of SAP YL and SAP PM offers a robust solution.

If you're looking to implement this integration in your organization or have experiences to share, feel free to leave a comment or ask any questions below. Stay tuned for more insights on how to optimize your SAP processes for greater business efficiency!

entry.title: Kit To Stock in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Warehouse management is at the core of efficient supply chain operations, and SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) plays a pivotal role in streamlining these processes. Among its many features, the Kit-to-Stock process stands out as a crucial mechanism for assembling products (kits) from individual components and stocking them for future use. This blog explores the purpose, process, and benefits of Kit-to-Stock in SAP EWM, with a practical example to bring the concept to life.

The Kit-to-Stock process involves assembling a final product (referred to as the Kit Header) from individual materials or parts (called Kit Components) and storing the assembled kit in the warehouse. This ensures that the product is ready for future use, whether it’s for fulfilling customer orders or internal production needs.

A Practical Example

Let’s break this down using a real-world scenario:

  • Kit Header: EV Charging Port Assembly
  • Kit Components: Charging Port, Connector Cable

In this scenario, the EV Charging Port Assembly (Kit Header) is the finished product created by combining components such as the Charging Port and Connector Cable. These components are picked from the warehouse, assembled, and stocked as a single unit.

Benefits of the Kit-to-Stock Process

  • Operational Efficiency: The process saves time by pre-assembling kits, reducing the need to handle individual components repeatedly.
  • Inventory Accuracy: Real-time tracking of kits and components ensures precise inventory management.
  • Flexibility: The process adapts to various scenarios, including bulk kit assembly for high-demand periods.

The Kit-to-Stock process in SAP EWM is a game-changer for warehouse management. By streamlining the assembly and stocking of kits, it enhances efficiency, reduces errors, and ensures readiness for customer demands or internal requirements. Whether you’re assembling EV Charging Ports or any other kits, this process offers the flexibility and accuracy modern warehouses need to stay competitive.

Let’s dive into configuration to make this scenario work.

Configuration:

1.   Define Order Type for VAS for KIT to stock


2.   Define Storage type for Work Center

Create a bin (KITS_BIN1) in Transaction /N/SCWM/LS01 and generate an activity area (KITS) for the specified storage type using transaction /N/SCWM/GENA. Additionally, perform bin sorting for the activity area using /N/SCWM/SBST. These steps have been omitted from this blog to maintain brevity.

3.   Process- Oriented Storage Control (POSC)

The Process-Oriented Storage Control (POSC) in the kitting process is configured to manage both key aspects of the operation.

Inbound Process: POSC applies to the kitting header product, ensuring that products are moved from the kitting work center and put away into their final storage bin, making them available for sales.

Outbound Process: POSC is configured to manage the picking of kitting components from the storage area and transferring them to the kitting work center.

External Storage Process Steps

Storage Process- Definition

Assign Storage Process step

Process-Oriented Storage Control

4.   Work Center

A Work Center in the warehouse is a designated area for performing specific physical activities, replicated in SAP EWM to reflect the actual workflow. This setup allows the system to recognize the activities carried out at the Work Center, enabling warehouse associates to update information directly into the system.

In the context of the kitting process, this Work Center differs from a regular work center. Since the kitting process involves both inbound and outbound operations configured in the POSC, the storage type associated with this Work Center has the role “I - Work Center with Staging Area Group.”(Refer Step-2). This role is essential as it facilitates posting of the Goods Issue for consuming kitting components and the Goods Receipt for receiving the kitting header product.

Define Work Center

SAP provides a standard Work Center Layout, "KITS," which can be utilized for the Kitting Work Center. This layout will be assigned to the external step defined in the POSC configuration.

Assign bin to work center- /N/SCWM/TWORKST

5.   Warehouse Order Creation Rule (WOCR)

The WOCR is essential for triggering POSC during the outbound process. In this scenario, the WOCR configuration also enables the automatic creation of Pick HUs. While WOCR offers a range of functionalities, this specific scenario focuses on utilizing the Packing Profile to facilitate the automatic Pick HU process.

  • Define Packing Profile for Warehouse Order Creation

For Auto Pick HU, There are more configuration to be done, listed down the steps but screenshots are omitted. if you are interested please refer the following link for detailed Configuration on WOCR
Link: https://www.stelliteworks.com/warehouse-order-creation-rule-wocr-in-sap-ewm-for-picking/

  • Packaging Specification with “0BDL- Work Order Creation” in transaction /N/SCWM/PACKSPEC.( Refer Step-11)
  • Assign Additional Attributes to Activity Area in transaction /N/SCWM/SEBA.
  • Define Warehouse Process Type for Pick HU with Internal Movement.
  • Assign pick HU WPT in Warehouse Number Control.

Define Creation Rule for Warehouse Order

Assign Outbound Storage Process which helps in triggering POSC in Outbound Process and assign Packing profile for auto pick HU.

6.   Warehouse Process Type

Warehouse Process Types need both inbound and outbound side and this also is used to trigger POSC in Inbound process.

  • Define Warehouse Process Type

Determine Warehouse Process Type

7.   Define Put away Strategies for Header Product after Kitting

Put away Strategies to put the finished product after kitting.

  • Define Storage type search sequence for put away

  • Assign Storage type to the Storage type search Sequence

  • Define Put away Control Indicator

  • Specify Storage type search sequence for Put away

8.    Stock Removal Strategies

Stock Removal Strategies to pick the components from storage area to  Kitting Work Center

  • Define Storage type search sequence for stock removal

  • Define Control Indicator for Search Sequence
  • Specify Storage type search sequence for Put away

9.   Bill Of Materials (BOM)

 10.  Packaging Specification

Pack Specification detailed configuration is skipped in this blog.

 

11.   Product Master

Header:

Components:

 

The Kit-to-Stock process is an integral part of Value-Added Services (VAS) in SAP EWM. The process begins with the creation and maintenance of VAS for Kit-to-Stock.

Maintain VAS- Kit to Stock- /N/SCWM/VAS_KTS

SAP provides the flexibility to create kits with or without a Bill of Materials (BOM)

  • With BOM: Utilizes an existing BOM already configured in the system.
  • Without BOM: Enables the addition of components directly to the header product.

In cases where BOMs are used, they are created using CS01, as shown in Step 9. The configuration involves assigning the order type, packaging specification, and the header product along with its quantity. This setup allows the creation of the VAS for Kit-to-Stock, as illustrated in Steps 1, 10, and 11 respectively.

Outbound and Inbound Deliveries in the Kitting Process

The Kit-to-Stock process involves both Outbound and Inbound deliveries

  • Outbound Delivery: Created to pick the components required for kitting from storage bins and transfer them to the kitting work center.
  • Inbound Delivery: Created in the header details of the kit to facilitate the put away of the final product back into storage bins after the kitting process.

 

Outbound & Inbound Deliveries

Outbound Delivery- /N/SCWM/PRDO

The Outbound Delivery created manually as see indicator in the image and it includes two line items representing the components required to form the kit. These line items are configured with the warehouse process type "KTSO", as per Step 6. Outbound delivery is to create picking warehouse tasks for picking the components from storage bins and transferring them to the kitting work center.

 

  • Inbound Delivery- /N/SCWM/PRDI

Inbound Delivery (Warehouse Request) is automatically created to facilitate the movement of the final product, assembled through the kitting process, to the designated storage bin. This ensures the product is ready for sale and can be efficiently shipped when an order is placed.

The Inbound Delivery contains only one line item, representing the final product. This line item is created with the warehouse process type “KTSI”, as illustrated in the image below. This process ensures seamless integration of the kitting output into the warehouse workflow, enabling quick availability for shipping.

 Creating Picking Warehouse Task for components for kitting

To create warehouse tasks for picking manually, the system selects the products from the ET10 storage type, as shown in the image below. These products are available in the bin ET10-10-14-04, which belongs to the ET10 storage type. The picking process is driven by the Storage Type Search Sequence for stock removal, configured in Step 8, ensuring that the system efficiently identifies and retrieves the required products.

Upon confirmation of the warehouse task, the products are transferred to the kitting work center bin KTS_BIN1. However, the system generated an additional warehouse task due to the configuration in Step 5, where auto pick HU was enabled. To proceed, filter the warehouse tasks using the process type “3070” and open the warehouse tasks in the monitor due to the configuration in Step-3.

Only one warehouse task was found because both products were picked in the same pick HU and moved together to KTS_BIN1. Upon confirmation, the products are transferred to the work center as shown in the below image. At the work center, associates perform the kitting process and update the system to reflect the completion of the activity.

Confirmation of VAS Order (Kit To Stock)-/N/SCWM/VASEXEC

 The products, along with their bin and VAS order details, can be viewed in this transaction. Under the Post Components section, if the HU is posted using the Post HU button, the system will perform a goods issue for those products in the outbound delivery, indicating that the products have been consumed for the kitting process.

Now Let’s pack them in new HU for processing

The system navigates to the packing work center, where the final product is packed into a new HU, as shown below. After packing, save the changes, and upon returning to the previous screen, the newly packed HU will be listed in the kitting work center bin.

When the newly packed HU containing the final product is posted by clicking the Post HU button, a Goods Receipt is recorded, indicating that the final product has been successfully created in the system.

Next, click on “Complete Process Step for HU” and save the changes. The system will automatically create a warehouse task (52273) to move the product from the work center to the designated storage bin, initiating the put away process.

A warehouse task is created to move the product from KTS_BIN1 to the E101-04-03 bin. This bin serves as the final storage location for the kit header product. Upon confirmation, the put away process will also be completed in the inbound delivery.

Let’s check the bin for final product

Finally, the kit product is available for sale with stock type F2. When an order for this product is received, it can be directly picked from the designated bin and shipped.

entry.title: Automatic Generation of Packaging item for Goods issue S4 HANA
entry.content:
Introduction:-

This blog includes configuration and master data set up to achieve automatic generation of packing items in delivery document.

Business requirement :-

When business need to have visibility of what packaging material used in deliveries as well as how many quantity of packing material has been consumed to prepare shipment. then we have to perform below stated configurations and master data setup to achieve business requirement.

 

To achieve requirement below configuration has to be set up.

Configuration

1. Define Packaging material types

SPRO--> Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Packing-->Define Packaging Material Types for Shipping.

2. Define packaging material group for packing material.

SPRO--> Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Packing-->Define Material Group for Packaging Materials

3. Define Allowed packing material types.

SPRO--> Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Packing-->Define Allowed Packaging Materials

4. Define packaging material at EWM

SPRO--> SCM Extended Warehouse Management-->Extended Warehouse Management-->Cross-Process Settings-->Handling Units-->Basics-->Define Packaging Material Types for Warehousing

5. Maintain Allowed Packaging Material Types for Packing Group

SPRO--> SCM Extended Warehouse Management-->Extended Warehouse Management-->Cross-Process Settings-->Handling Units-->Basics-->Maintain Allowed Packaging Material Types for Packing Group

6. Assign number ranges for Packaging material

SPRO--> SCM Extended Warehouse Management-->Extended Warehouse Management-->Cross-Process Settings-->Handling Units-->External Identification-->Assign Number Range Intervals to Packaging Material Types

7. Activate automatic generation of packaging items.

SPRO--> SCM Extended Warehouse Management-->Extended Warehouse Management-->Goods Issue Process-->Outbound Delivery-->Automatically Generate Packaging Items.

Activate Delivery item for combination of warehouse and Delivery type.

select warehouse line and double click on Relevance of Pack materials.

Select line and click on Determination of Delivery Item Type and maintain delivery type item type and stock type

in this configuration assign packing material to EWM document type and item type OPHU and select stock type as F2.

8. For ECC delivery type activate automatic packaging indicator.

SPRO-->Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Deliveries-->Define Delivery Types.

9. Assign packaging material  item categories to delivery type with usage of PACK.

SPRO-->Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Deliveries-->Define Item Category Determination in Deliveries.

Master Data set up

1. Create Packaging material and assign packaging material type and group in sales general/plant data view what we have created in above configuration

2. If Packaging material is inventory managed then goods movement bin should be maintained in transaction

/SCWM/GMBIN_DET.

Test case:-

  • Create sales order by using transaction Tcode VA01.
  • Create delivery with reference to sales order using Tcode VL01N.
  • Perform Picking activity in EWM
  • During Packing select packing material EP1RA-01 to perform packing in transaction /SCWM/PACK for EWM delivery.
  • Performing Goods issue from EWM deliver.

After Goods issue ODO contains packing material along with quantity

in ECC delivery also packaging material will appear

 

 

Conclusion:-

By Performing all above configuration and master data set up packaging material will start populating in EWM delivery as well as in ECC delivery with quantity which has been consumed perform packing in work-center.
